original_text:
### Hierarchy & Application
- Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.
- **Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
- **Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).
- **Charts/about:blank** Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.
- **Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
- **Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.
- **Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.
- The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

relations.note: The Coral restriction specifies the permitted tertiary-accent role within the overall palette hierarchy.
Both rules constrain color use in data visualizations and data emphasis.

quotes: Apply the palette in the **primary > secondary > tertiary ratio** shown in the guide: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents and surfaces.

rules.intent: Maintain the intended visual hierarchy of the brand palette.
rules.rule_text: Apply the palette in the primary > secondary > tertiary ratio: blues and golds dominate, while tertiary colors are used as accents and surfaces.

quotes: **Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.
**Headlines/headings:** Brand Blue (or White on dark/gradient backgrounds).
**Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).
**Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Preserve readable, contrast-appropriate text and link treatments across backgrounds.
rules.rule_text: Use the headline color treatment (Brand Blue #00529b, with White on dark or gradient backgrounds), body color treatment (Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds and White on dark backgrounds), and increased body line height on dark backgrounds. Links use the Brand Blue #00529b treatment and are underlined on light backgrounds, with White underlined links on dark backgrounds.

quotes: **Charts/about:blank** Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.
**Charts/about:blank** Brand Blue + Sky Blue dominant; **Sunshine/Gold strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures**; gray for placebo.

rules.intent: Keep chart data hierarchy clear and reserve warm colors for high-value emphasis.
rules.rule_text: Charts use Brand Blue #00529b and Sky Blue #358ccb as dominant colors. Sunshine #faa31b and Gold #ffc60a are strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures; use gray for placebo.

quotes: **Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
**Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).

rules.intent: Differentiate data callouts from text-heavy callouts with approved surfaces.
rules.rule_text: Data callouts use the Light Blue #e6f0f9 surface treatment at 70% tint; text-heavy callouts use the Golden Sand #fef1c8 surface treatment at 30% tint.

quotes: **Coral:** sparing tertiary accent only — never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. **[GENERAL]:** avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it.

rules.intent: Prevent Coral from displacing primary hierarchy or data emphasis.
rules.rule_text: Use Coral #f26a38 only as a sparing tertiary accent; never use it for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. In email sections, avoid Coral unless the brief calls for it.

quotes: The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).
The logo icon's **70% transparency of Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a must be maintained in all instances** (logo, and when the mark is used as a pattern/graphic).

rules.intent: Ensure consistent logo-mark rendering across all uses.
rules.rule_text: Maintain the logo mark's 70% transparency in Gold (Pantone 7548 C / #ffc60a) in every instance, including the logo and the mark when used as a pattern or graphic.

quotes: - **Body text:** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2).
- **Callout surfaces:** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts).
- **Links [GENERAL]:** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark.

rules.intent: Bind the Hierarchy & Application table values verbatim.
rules.rule_text:
The Hierarchy & Application table is a closed, brand-approved set; every row binds its listed values exactly as specified. Do not invent or alter rows.

| item | value |
|---|---|
| Body text | ** Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds; White on dark backgrounds (with increased line height, §1.2). |
| Callout surfaces | ** Light Blue at 70% tint (data callouts); Golden Sand at 30% tint (text-heavy callouts). |
| Links [GENERAL] | ** Brand Blue #00529b, underlined, on light backgrounds; White underlined on dark. |
